{"step_by_step_thinking": "The child has received full Rabies vaccination in December 2018, which means they have completed the primary vaccination series. According to the documents, for previously vaccinated patients, only rabies vaccine is required after an exposure assessed to be at risk of rabies. The documents do not mention the need for RIG (rabies immune globulin) for previously vaccinated patients unless the exposure is severe or the patient is immunocompromised. Since the pet also had vaccination, it suggests the exposure might not be as severe. Therefore, the next line of management would be 2 doses of Rabies vaccine.", "answer_choice": "D"}
